frameworks.name: deep RNN / connectionist sensorimotor model
frameworks.one_line: RNN trained on psychophysical tasks with language-embedded instruction inputs

frameworks.name: pre-trained language model embeddings
frameworks.one_line: NLP embeddings used to scaffold task representations for zero-shot generalization

frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: framework for combining practiced skills into novel task performance

core_question: How can a neural model use linguistic instructions to perform novel sensorimotor tasks with zero prior experience?
key_assumptions: language embeddings carry task-relevant semantic structure
pre-trained LM provides instruction representations without task-specific tuning
manipulations: instruction embedding source (pre-trained language model variants)
key_findings: best models reach 83% correct on unseen tasks from instructions alone (0-shot)
neural activity for related tasks shares geometry with instruction semantic embeddings
model can generate linguistic descriptions of novel tasks from motor feedback
generated descriptions guide a partner model to perform the same task
interpretations: language scaffolds sensorimotor representations so instructions cue composition of practiced skills
shared geometry between semantic and neural task spaces enables linguistic communication of novel tasks
